'''Mark McLinden''' (born 8 July 1979) is an Australian former professional [[rugby football]]er of the 1990s and 2000s. He played [[rugby league]] in the [[National Rugby League|NRL]] for Australian club, the [[Canberra Raiders]] and in the [[Super League]] for English club the [[London Broncos#1994–2005: Broncos and Super League|London Broncos]]/[[London Broncos#2006–2011: Harlequins Rugby League|Harlequins RL]], which he also captained. A [[Country New South Wales rugby league team|Country New South Wales]] representative backline player, McLinden finished his career in 2009 with a few [[rugby union]] games for the [[Queensland Reds]] in [[Super Rugby]].

==Early years==
McLinden was born in [[Canberra]], [[Australian Capital Territory|ACT]], and played his early rugby at West Belconnen Warriors in the Canberra District Junior Rugby League. While attending Hawker College, McLinden played for the [[Australia national schoolboys rugby league team|Australian Schoolboys team]] in 1996 and 1997.<ref>{{cite web|title=SportingPulse Homepage for Australian Secondary Schools Rugby League|url=http://www.sportingpulse.com/assoc_page.cgi?c=7-2130-0-0-0&sID=26424|accessdate=2008-10-10|publisher=SportingPulse|archive-date=21 August 2008|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20080821104534/http://www.sportingpulse.com/assoc_page.cgi?c=7-2130-0-0-0&sID=26424|url-status=dead}}</ref>

==Rugby league career==
In 1998, McLinden signed to play in the [[National Rugby League|NRL]] for his local team [[Canberra Raiders]]. Following an impressive début season, Mark was named [[National Rugby League|NRL]] Rookie of the Year,<ref name="From Canberra to Brentford">{{cite news|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/super_league/london/4251315.stm|title=From Canberra to Brentford|publisher=BBC|date=2005-09-12|access-date=2007-07-31|first=Phil|last=Harlow}}</ref> and later won the Raiders' player of the year award.<ref>{{Cite web |title=1992–2000 |url=https://www.raiders.com.au/about/season-summaries/1992-2000/ |access-date=2022-10-04 |website=Canberra Raiders |language=en}}</ref>

He also received representative honours with selection for the Australian [[Junior Kangaroos]] squad to play tests against the [[Junior Kiwis]]. In 2001 McLinden was selected to play in the [[City vs Country Origin]] for Country. During his seven seasons at the [[Canberra Raiders]], McLinden made 165 appearances, featuring in five play-off series.

===Super League===
[[File:Mark McLinden.jpg|250px|thumb|left|McLinden in action for Harlequins RL]]
McLinden signed for the [[London Broncos#1994–2005: Broncos and Super League|London Broncos]] in November 2004<ref name="Broncos sign Raiders scrum-half">{{cite news|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/super_league/london/3992033.stm|title=Broncos sign Raiders scrum-half|publisher=BBC|date=2004-11-08|access-date=2007-07-31}}</ref> and was appointed team captain following on from [[Jim Dymock]].<ref name="Broncos sign Raiders scrum-half"/> During 2005's [[Super League X]], his first in the [[Super League]], Mark showed his versatility by appearing at {{rlp|fb|hb}} or {{rlp|hk}}.

In 2006's [[Super League XI]], with his club now operating as the [[London Broncos#2006–2011: Harlequins Rugby League|Harlequins RL]], Mark was the club's leading Super League try scorer with 14 tries from 17 appearances. His season was, however, disrupted by a persistent back injury which required an operation in late 2006.<ref name="Harlequins suffer triple setback">{{cite news|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport1/hi/rugby_league/super_league/london/6161219.stm|title=Harlequins suffer triple setback|publisher=BBC|date=2006-12-08|access-date=2007-07-31}}</ref>

McLinden had played much of his career at {{rlp|hb}}, and with the arrival of [[Great Britain national rugby league team|Great Britain]] international {{rlp|fb}} [[Chris Melling (rugby league)|Chris Melling]] at the [[London Broncos#2006–2011: Harlequins Rugby League|Harlequins RL]] he played several games of 2007's [[Super League XII]] in the halves.

The 2007 [[Super League XII]] saw McLinden return from injury. However, he was replaced as captain by [[England national rugby league team|England]] international [[Rob Purdham]].<ref name="Harlequins & Hull name skippers">{{cite news|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/super_league/6286991.stm|title=Harlequins & Hull name skippers|publisher=BBC|date=2007-01-22|access-date=2007-07-31}}</ref>

McLinden signed a three-year extension to his contract to keep him at Harlequins until the end of the 2009 season. However, in 2008 he suffered from a long debilitating illness which caused him to lose a lot of weight. As a result, he barely featured in Super League and he was released at the end of the year.

Before his switch to rugby union he was asked to play for the [[Ireland national rugby league team]] at the [[2008 Rugby League World Cup|2008 World Cup]], but declined in favour of Super 14 pre-season training.<ref name="Stuff.co.nz_691907">{{cite news|url=http://www.stuff.co.nz/sport/league/691907|title=Super 14 shot put ahead of League World Cup|date=28 October 2008|agency=[[Australian Associated Press|AAP]]|work=[[Stuff.co.nz]]|accessdate=20 October 2011}}</ref>

==Rugby union career==
McLinden played [[rugby union]] for the [[Queensland Reds]] in 2009. He scored his first [[Super Rugby|Super 14]] try in his side's 22–3 win over South Africa's [[Cheetahs (rugby union)|Cheetahs]] at [[Lang Park|Suncorp Stadium]]. Just before the 2009/10 Super 14 season he retired from the game due to medical advice.<ref>{{Cite web |date=2009-11-10 |title=Mark McLinden announces retirement |url=https://www.stuff.co.nz/sport/rugby/super-rugby/3049969/Mark-McLinden-announces-retirement |access-date=2022-10-04 |website=Stuff |language=en}}</ref>

==Later life==
McLinden is a co-director of Animalates, an exercise program for children aged 4–11, combining [[Pilates]], [[yoga]], [[aerobics]] and [[dance]] with an animal characters theme.<ref>[http://www.osteopathy.org.au/data/Exercise__Diet_in_Children__Adolescents_with_Diabetes.pdf osteopathy.org]; Retrieved 20 September 2013</ref>

McLinden was arrested for a [[pitch invasion]] at the [[2022 NRL Grand Final]]. He entered the field wearing a shirt with "end coal, gas & oil" on the front and "for our kids" on the back, in protest against Government inaction to prevent [[Climate change in Australia|climate change]].<ref>{{Cite web |last=Chammas |first=Michael |date=2022-10-04 |title=Former Raiders star arrested after pitch invasion in grand final |url=https://www.smh.com.au/sport/nrl/former-raiders-star-arrested-after-pitch-invasion-in-grand-final-20221004-p5bn3n.html |access-date=2022-10-04 |website=The Sydney Morning Herald |language=en}}</ref> McLinden was fined $5000 and banned from future NRL games.<ref>{{Cite web |date=2022-10-04 |title=Grand final pitch invader identified as ex-NRL star after his wild protest ends with huge fine |url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/nrl-2022-grand-final-pitch-invader-mark-mclinden-canberra-raiders-fined-former-player-5000-accor-stadium-5000/news-story/09b156f477460705a57b37c892346cd7 |access-date=2022-10-04 |work=Fox Sports |language=en}}</ref>

McLinden threatened to disrupt the 2023 NRL Grand Final, via a tip-off to a Journalist from
The Sydney Morning Herald. Rather than run the story, the journalist tipped off the NSW Police and the NRL. Security and officials carried pictures of McLinden ahead of the match, in the hope of identifying him before he attempted his pitch invasion.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Chammas |first=Michael |date=2023-10-01 |title='Gonna go again': Police and security prepared for another McLinden pitch invasion |url=https://www.smh.com.au/sport/nrl/no-sleep-til-brisbane-broncos-kept-awake-on-game-eve-by-music-festival-20230930-p5e8t5.html |access-date=2024-06-07 |website=The Sydney Morning Herald |language=en}}</ref>
